# Seller Shipping and Fulfillment

This policy explains your responsibilities for shipping orders to customers, including handling time, tracking, and packaging.

## Handling Time

- Confirm and ship orders within the handling time set on your listings (default is 1–2 business days).
- If you cannot meet the handling time, update your inventory or handling settings rather than letting orders go late.

## Tracking

- Upload valid carrier tracking information for every shipped order.
- Mark orders as shipped only after they are genuinely dispatched.
- Valid, scannable tracking protects you in delivery disputes and supports your performance metrics.

## Packaging

- Package items securely to prevent damage in transit.
- Use packaging appropriate to the product's fragility, size, and value.

## Delivery Promises

- The estimated delivery date shown to the customer is based on your handling time plus carrier transit time.
- Communicate proactively through the order if a delay occurs.

## Lost or Damaged Shipments

- For shipments lost or damaged in transit, work with the carrier and the customer to resolve the issue promptly, including reshipment or refund where appropriate.

## International Shipping

- If you offer international shipping, you are responsible for correct customs documentation and for clearly stating any duties the customer may owe.
